bottom_line: Make QV gates scope-aware so unrelated/out-of-scope failures do not consume all fix cycles.
coverage_notes: DB telemetry available (iw db-identity check: UP). All item logs in ai-dev/logs for I-00117 were under 1 MB; logs were read directly (no tail/head sampling required). Prompt and manifest were also reviewed.

findings.title: QV assertion gate deadlocked on out-of-scope failures
findings.recommendation: Add pre-check logic in QV/fix-cycle flow to detect out-of-scope failing files and escalate/amend scope instead of retrying the same gate.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00117_S09_run12.log:6 — 'Failed I-00117 step S09: assertions failed: exit=2'
ai-dev/logs/I-00117_S09_fix5.log:106 — 'fails, but only due to out-of-scope file'
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Add scope-aware QV retry handling so out-of-scope failing files trigger escalation/scope amendment instead of repeated fix cycles; analyzed in I-00117 (see ai-dev/work/I-00117/reports/I-00117_self_assess_report.md).

findings.title: Duplicate successful reruns after step already completed
findings.recommendation: Enforce idempotency guard before launching a run (skip if step already terminal/completed) and log dedupe reason.
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add daemon idempotency guard to prevent duplicate reruns after a step is already completed; analyzed in I-00117 (see ai-dev/work/I-00117/reports/I-00117_self_assess_report.md).
